bertscore.original_cuda_error:
CUDA error: no kernel image is available for execution on the device
Search for `cudaErrorNoKernelImageForDevice' in https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__TYPES.html for more information.
CUDA kernel errors might be asynchronously reported at some other API call, so the stacktrace below might be incorrect.
For debugging consider passing CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1
Compile with `TORCH_USE_CUDA_DSA` to enable device-side assertions.

per_entity_results.generated: Performance Evaluation of MoonGen and L2 Forwarder Latency Measurements on NIDA and CESIS Nodes
per_entity_results.reference: MoonGen Load Latency Benchmarks

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset comprises measurements of network latency performed using the MoonGen load generation tool and an L2 forwarder component, libmoon. The experiment involved establishing a Digital Front-End Test (DUT) environment across nodes nida and cesis through automated scripts that cloned software libraries and configured virtual file systems. Multiple configurations of packet rates and sizes were utilized to assess system performance under simulated workload conditions. Evaluation outputs include CSV logs of latency data and associated image plots, generated by the load generation script, providing insights into resource utilization and potential bottlenecks within the libmoon forwarding process.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment measures l2-load-latency in a two-node setup with MoonGen on one node as the load generator and libmoon on another node as the forwarding device under test. The benchmark tests different packet rates and packet sizes. Each measurement runs for fixed duration, records energy consumption on both machines, and stores latency histograms as CSV data and PNG plots for every parameter combination.

per_entity_results.generated: This script establishes a Digital Front-End Test (DUT) environment by cloning a Git repository containing the libmoon software library. The process involves building the library and configuring a hugetlbfs virtual file system, alongside disabling Intel Turbo Boost functionality. Synchronization of the build steps is ensured through the execution of shell commands within the defined environment.
per_entity_results.reference: Prepares the DUT node by building libmoon and enabling hugepage support. It also disables CPU turbo boost for more stable forwarding measurements.

per_entity_results.generated: This script establishes a load generation environment utilizing the MoonGen tool, facilitating performance testing of system resources. The process involves cloning the source code repository and executing build and configuration steps to prepare the system for simulated workload. Synchronization is achieved through the execution of shell commands related to package installation and repository checkout.
per_entity_results.reference: Prepares the load-generator node by building MoonGen from the provided repository and branch. Additionally, it enables hugepage support for packet generation and latency measurements.

per_entity_results.generated: File copy from /home/warmuth/experiments/experiments/loop-experiment-llm/evaluation/plot_latency.py to MoonGen/plot_latency.py on node cesis.
per_entity_results.reference: File copy from /home/warmuth/experiments/experiments/loop-experiment-llm/evaluation/plot_latency.py to MoonGen/plot_latency.py on node cesis.

per_entity_results.generated: The command executed a load generation test to measure latency.
per_entity_results.reference: Runs MoonGen's l2-load-latency example using the selected transmit and receive ports and histogram output paths.

per_entity_results.generated: This script implements a load generation experiment to measure network latency, utilizing a Lua-based application and configurable packet rates and sizes. The execution synchronizes with other nodes via `pos_sync` and captures timing data for analysis, generating a CSV log of latencies. Subsequently, the script produces an image plot of the latency distribution and uploads both the raw data and generated visualization to the system.
per_entity_results.reference: Looped benchmark that iterates over all configured packet-rate and packet-size combinations, synchronizes with the DUT, records node energy usage and runs MoonGen's l2-load-latency example. Afterwards it uploads the generated latency histogram and plot.

per_entity_results.generated: This iteration executes a load generation benchmark to evaluate network latency characteristics. The script adjusts packet rate and packet size parameters, targeting a specified port range for transmission and reception. Recorded output includes a CSV file containing latency histograms generated from the load generation process.
per_entity_results.reference: Each iteration runs the MoonGen l2-load-latency script with different values for packet rate and packet size, storing the measured latencies in a CSV file, generating a plot, and uploading both artifacts after each run.

per_entity_results.generated: The command executed a Lua script to simulate L2 forwarding latency.
per_entity_results.reference: Runs libmoon's l2-forward example so the DUT forwards traffic during the load-generator measurement window.

per_entity_results.generated: This script facilitates latency measurements of an L2 forwarder component, designated as libmoon, by executing a Lua-based example program. The experiment synchronizes execution and captures energy consumption data during the forwarding process, utilizing specified packet rates and sizes.  The script then terminates the test instance and associated processes to conclude the measurement cycle.
per_entity_results.reference: Forwarder that synchronizes with the load-generator node, records energy usage and forwards the received packets back to the load-generator node.

per_entity_results.generated: This script executes a latency benchmark for an L2 forwarder, utilizing configurable packet rates and sizes to assess network performance. The primary recorded output tracks the measured latency of data transmission through the simulated device. Parameter variations include packet rate (100 packets/second) and packet size (64 bytes).
per_entity_results.reference: Each iteration runs the libmoon l2-forward script to forward the received packets from the loadgen node.
